content_of_post_to_comment:
(sorry about any mistakes or errors as im writing this im a wreck also im not sure if this is the right subreddit so mods feel free to insta delete)

Hello, I'm a 22m third-year student at a university in the UK who has failed and am in the process of retaking assignments and an exam. This isn't the first time it's happened to me. I flunked my second year and had to repeat it, and during my final year at college, I had to redo assignments.

I come from an okay-ish background. I'm the youngest of 3, two older sisters (32,30), the oldest kind of a bum like myself and the middle a UCL undergrad and master's student now looking to do her PhD. When i was younger my parents had paid for maths science and english tutors for my GCSE's which did help and i was able to get okay ish grades however, at the start of year 11(2019/2020) i started to lose all motivation and my aspirations my friends and teachers all started to say i developed a non-chalant attitude and stoped caring which looking back i did and ive carried this same attitude for the past 6 years and it continues to be my downfall.

First year of uni went great, I made friends, passed with flying colours, it was great, I had the "uni experience" that everyone talks about. I wasn't able to find a house with my friends, so my second year led me into private accommodation. I lived in a flat of 7, but that was the loneliest I've ever felt. I stopped talking to half the friends I made in year 1 and the 2 I still had didn't live anywhere near me, but we still interacted because we were on the same course. I hated everything, the same feeling I felt during secondary school came back. I stopped caring, got lazy and thought to myself, everything would be fine if I wasn't here anymore. So my parents paid for counselling, and it helped, but I foolishly stopped because the happiness I felt was different in the 21 years of my life, I never felt free before.

Last summer, after repeating my second year, I had a positive outlook on my future. I was gonna finish the last year and graduate like everyone else and start my career. December comes around, and the mood tanks. I get lazy, and I stop caring about everything. I think the change came when I answered a question wrong during my lectures, which made people chuckle. This crashed my mental health. To this day, I feel stupid, like I don't belong, like these people are better than me. So I stopped trying. I noticed that whenever I asked for help, my lecturers ignored me. 30 mins would go by before they acknowledged me, ending up with me not going into class anymore leading to the situation im in right noe

I've skipped a lot of the details because I can't form my thoughts correctly atm but I'm asking for advice on what to do next. What can I do to stop this mental health crash i get yearly and this feeling like I don't belong? How do I stop this "non-chalantness" that ive developed? How can i turn my future around so i can still live the life i want.
comment: Getting laughed at in a lecture and then pulling away from classes sounds like it set off a loop of avoiding anything that might repeat that feeling. When you stop going, everything piles up and it gets heavier. If you’re still enrolled, I’d try rebuilding just one fixed routine point a day (one lecture or office hour) and lean on uni support services rather than doing it alone. Have you spoken to student support about how this keeps cycling?
